perturbation.original_text: Within ten (10) days after award and/or completion of the IFB, or upon demand by the County , all Confidential Information, including all photographs, sketches, models, and drawings related to the desi gn, layout and operation of said project shall be destroyed and files deleted from all of the Bidder’s web sites and electronic devices (including but not limited to computers, cellular phones, etc.).
perturbation.changed_text: Within ten (10) days after award and/or completion of the IFB, or upon demand by the County , all Confidential Information, including all photographs, sketches, models, and drawings related to the desi gn, layout and operation of said project will be retained and files saved to all of the Bidder’s web sites and electronic devices (including but not limited to computers, cellular phones, etc.).
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires the bidder to destroy all confidential information after completion. This revision mandates they *retain* it and save it to their systems, directly contradicting the confidentiality and data destruction clauses. This could lead to data breaches and legal disputes over information control.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure The Bidder shall not publish, or make and/or retain copies or disclose to any third party, any Confidential Information furnished to the Bidder except to such extent as may be necessary to carry out the Bidder ’s duties under this agreement and except to the extent that disclosure is required by judicial process in a legal proceeding, in which event Bidder will provide to the County notice of the request or demand to disclose Confidential Information. Within ten (10) days after award and/or completion of the IFB, or upon demand by the County , all Confidential Information, including all photographs, sketches, models, and drawings related to the desi gn, layout and operation of said project will be retained and files saved to all of the Bidder’s web sites and electronic devices (including but not limited to computers, cellular phones, etc.).
perturbation.justification: The original text required the bidder to destroy all confidential information after completion of the IFB, while the changed text mandates they retain it and save it to their systems. This directly contradicts the requirement to destroy the information. The contradicted text reinforces the bidder's duty not to retain copies of confidential information, which is directly violated by the changed text instructing them to retain and save the files. Thus, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Bidder agrees to indemnify and h old harmless the County, its elected officials, employees and agents from and against any and all liability, damages, claims, suits, liens, and judgments (including reasonable attorney’s fees), of whatever nature, for injuries to or death of any person or persons, or loss of or damage to property, to the extent attributable to the negligent acts of Bidder , its subcontractors or its respective agents, servants, or employees or such parties’ related to said Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Bidder is not required to indemnify or hold harmless the County, its elected officials, employees, and agents from and against any liability, damages, claims, suits, liens, and judgments, of whatever nature, even if attributable to the negligent acts of Bidder, its subcontractors or its respective agents, servants, or employees related to said Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires the bidder to indemnify the County against damages related to negligence concerning confidential information. The changed text explicitly states the bidder is *not* required to indemnify, even in cases of negligence. This creates a direct contradiction and removes legal protection for the County.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Indemnification Bidder is not required to indemnify or hold harmless the County, its elected officials, employees, and agents from and against any liability, damages, claims, suits, liens, and judgments (including reasonable attorney’s fees), of whatever nature, even if attributable to the negligent acts of Bidder , its subcontractors or its respective agents, servants, or employees related to said Confidential Information. 4. Confidentiality A. “Confiden tial Information” as outlined herein shall be used in strict confidence solely for the purpose allotted under this agreement and shall not be disclosed by the Bidder , its employee s, partners, subcontractors, agents, or clients to a third party without the prior written consent of the County with the exception of the following;
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Bidder agrees to indemnify the County for damages related to negligence concerning confidential information. The changed text explicitly states the Bidder is *not* required to indemnify, even in cases of negligence. This is a direct contradiction. The 'Contradicted Text' re-iterates the changed text, reinforcing the contradiction since the change text completely negates the original indemnity agreement, leading to a clear and strong inconsistency within the document, which is considered a structural flaw, per the definitions given.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information means all information relating to the design, layout and operation of said Project .
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information means some information relating to the design, layout and operation of said Project.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "all information" to "some information," the definition of Confidential Information is made ambiguous. The change directly introduces uncertainty, as it is unclear what specific information is considered confidential. This undermines the entire purpose of the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. Confidential Information means some information relating to the design, layout and operation of said Project . 5. Governing Law This agreement validity, construction and effect shall be governed under the substantive law of the Commonwealth of Virginia without the giving effect to any choice of law provision. Bidder expressly waives any objection to venue or jurisdiction of the L oudoun County Circuit Court, Loudoun County, Virginia.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines Confidential Information as 'all information relating to the design, layout and operation of said Project.' The changed text modifies this to 'some information relating to the design, layout and operation of said Project.' The contradicted text also states 'B. Confidential Information means some information relating to the design, layout and operation of said Project.' While the contradicted text and changed text are the same, the change from 'all' to 'some' creates ambiguity because it is unclear which specific information is considered confidential. Because both statements (the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text) are in the same document, the Change from 'all information' to 'some information' does create a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement constitutes the complete integration of all oral and written documents, is the entire and final agreement between the parties and may be amended only in writing signed by authorized officials of both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement does not constitutes the complete integration of all oral and written documents, is not the entire and final agreement between the parties and may be amended orally without the signed by authorized officials of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes that the written agreement is the complete and final agreement. The modification states that agreement is not final, it can be changed orally, contradicting original understanding and creating a problem in enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement The governing terms and conditions of this agreement are expressly limited to the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement. This agreement does not constitutes the complete integration of all oral and written documents, is not the entire and final agreement between the parties and may be amended orally without the signed by authorized officials of both parties. 7. Authority to Bind Firm in Agreement Person signing Agreement will sh ow title or authority to bind the firm in an agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies the agreement is the 'entire and final' and can only be amended in writing. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating it 'is not the entire and final agreement' and can be amended orally. This directly conflicts with the 'Entire Agreement' clause in the contradicted text, creating a clear and strong contradiction regarding the terms of the agreement, enforceability, and what constitutes a valid modification.
